reasoning:
The forecast horizon is extremely short (May 12, 2025, to June 1, 2025—a gap of 20 days). While Ramzan Kadyrov floated resignation rumors and formally requested to be relieved of his post on May 5–6, 2025, this matches his established historical pattern of offering his resignation to secure public validation or reaffirm his standing with the Kremlin (as he similarly did in 2022). 

On May 7, 2025, Kadyrov met with President Vladimir Putin in Moscow. Following this meeting, the Institute for the Study of War (ISW) and other analytical outlets reported that Putin rejected the resignation request, effectively signaling an expectation that Kadyrov will continue to govern Chechnya to maintain regional stability. Kadyrov's response was a pledge of continued loyalty, stating, "We will not let you down." 

Although Kadyrov is dealing with severe health complications (including documented reports of pancreatic necrosis), the fact that he was healthy enough to travel to the Kremlin for a televised, official meeting strongly suggests he is not in imminent risk of death over the next few weeks. Furthermore, any immediate transition is complicated by the fact that his leading successor, his son Adam, is just 17 years old. With Putin explicitly rejecting his request to step down, the probability of Kadyrov leaving office or dying before June 1 is incredibly low. I assign the minimum allowable bound of 5%.
